mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.02 over 3-year rolling windows or positive return frequency drops below 55% during rate cuts.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that trigger immediate risk-off positioning, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE increases by more than 0.2 percentage points month-over-month
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in more than 60% of triggered instances or the signal loses statistical significance with p > 0.10 in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates broad-based consumer demand strength, which drives corporate earnings growth and yields positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in RSAFS and HOUST fail to produce a positive return frequency above 58% over 60-day horizons, or the interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indices reflect expanding manufacturing capacity and supply-side momentum, which supports equity valuations and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days during mid-cycle expansions.
failure_condition: The signal produces negative 20-day returns in over 50% of observations when INDPRO growth exceeds 1% quarter-over-quarter, or regime-specific IC falls below zero.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in total nonfarm payrolls reflect strengthening economic activity and wage growth, which directly supports consumer spending and generates positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive payroll revisions consistently coincide with negative 5-day forward returns, or the rank correlation between payroll changes and 5-day returns remains below 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation above a 0.4% monthly threshold forces expectations of tighter monetary policy, which compresses equity multiples and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL spikes above the threshold consistently precede positive 60-day returns, or the inflation threshold fails to separate return distributions with a p-value below 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between quarterly GDP growth and monthly personal consumption expenditures signals underlying demand resilience that outpaces headline output, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction between GDPC1 and RSAFS growth rates yields a negative or insignificant coefficient in predictive regressions, or 20-day returns are negative in more than 55% of aligned periods.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during periods of high housing inventory signals supply-side stagnation and weak capital expenditure, which triggers immediate risk aversion and negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Negative INDPRO growth during high HOUST periods fails to predict negative 5-day returns, or the signal predictive accuracy drops below random chance in walk-forward validation.
